title: My Friend Sent the Wrong Screenshot to the Wrong Person
hook: She tried to roast me in a private chat, then accidentally sent the receipts straight back to me.

scenes.text: She texted me like everything was normal, but five minutes later I got a screenshot that was clearly meant for somebody else, and my name was right there in the middle of the joke.
scenes.visualDirection: Open on the wrong-message beat immediately and make the message UI the main attraction.

scenes.text: Instead of apologizing, she unsent one line, started acting casual, and pretended I had imagined the whole thing even though I had already saved the screenshot.
scenes.visualDirection: Use message updates, unsent cues, and timing tension rather than generic filler.

scenes.text: So I replied with the exact screenshot she thought I never saw and asked if she wanted to restart the conversation with the truth this time.
scenes.visualDirection: Build toward a visible receipt-drop reveal.

scenes.text: She panicked, sent three different excuses, then blamed me for being dramatic when all I did was quote her own words back to her.
scenes.visualDirection: Keep the panic beat inside the message flow and light support motion.

scenes.text: Now half our friend group says I embarrassed her on purpose, but if you lie in screenshots you should expect screenshots back, so tell me if I went too far.
scenes.visualDirection: End on a judgment beat with one last clean receipt.

reasoning: Reference shape only. Keep the same message-led reveal structure.
cta: Was I petty, or just honest?
